comment: Registry of all datasets in the Lux Mentis pipeline. Used by health_check.py to run structural conformance checks. Do not edit provenance fields by hand â€” they are updated by the scripts that produce each output.

known_anomalies.reason: SNF enrollment filter — all rows are PART A SNF (00-18). Constant by design.
SNF enrollment filter — all rows are PART A PROVIDER - SKILLED NURSING FACILITY. Constant by design.
Address line 2 is sparse by design; most US addresses don't use it. 95.7% null is expected.

datasets.name: snf_owners_raw
datasets.label: SNF Owners â€” Raw (gitignored, individual names)

datasets.name: pos_iqies
datasets.label: POS iQIES â€” All Provider Types

known_anomalies.reason: CMS batch processing date — constant within a vintage pull. Not a data field.

datasets.name: cms_enrollments_all_types
datasets.label: CMS Enrollments â€” All Medicare Provider Types

datasets.name: facility_master
datasets.label: Facility Master â€” Spine with Geography

known_anomalies.reason: CMS batch processing date — constant within a vintage pull.
Census vintage label — constant across the ZCTA join (2024-5yr). Expected.

datasets.name: leie
datasets.label: OIG LEIE Exclusions (gitignored, individual names)
datasets.note: OIG exclusions list â€” individual names, DOBs. Role-gated. Never public.

known_anomalies.reason: Census vintage label — constant for a single ACS pull. Expected.

datasets.name: nh_provider_info
datasets.label: NH Provider Info (Five-Star + Staffing + SFF)
datasets.schema_note: CMS-3442-F threshold flags computed by script (counterfactual — rule repealed Dec 2025); lm_meets_3442f_thresholds=False for 74.5% in current CSV (PENDING regeneration — script threshold was corrected). See CORRECTIONS.md.

known_anomalies.reason: SFF status is sparse by design. Only ~530 of 14,695 national facilities carry SFF or SFF-candidate status (~3.6%). 96%+ null is expected distribution, not missing data.

Boolean field (True/False string) — numeric hint heuristic (_hprd) is a false positive. Computed by script from HPRD values; True/False strings are not parseable as numeric. Documented in CORRECTIONS.md.

Boolean field (True/False string) — numeric hint heuristic (_hprd) is a false positive. Computed by script from HPRD values; True/False strings are not parseable as numeric. Documented in CORRECTIONS.md.

Boolean field (True/False string) — numeric hint heuristic (_hprd) is a false positive. Computed by script from HPRD values; True/False strings are not parseable as numeric. Documented in CORRECTIONS.md.

Null in current output CSV — CSV was generated by an earlier script version before these fields were computed. Will be non-null (True/False) after regeneration; this null_rate suppression becomes vacuous post-regeneration.
Same as lm_rn_meets_3442f null_rate — null in current CSV, will be non-null after regeneration.
Same as lm_rn_meets_3442f null_rate — null in current CSV, will be non-null after regeneration.

Boolean field (True/False string) — numeric hint heuristic (_hprd) is a false positive. Computed by script from cna_hprd >= 2.45 (CMS-3442-F nurse aide minimum). New field added by threshold correction; not in current output CSV until regenerated.
Health deficiency file contains only F-tags (F000-F999). K-tags and E-tags are in the fire safety file. tag_prefix='F' is expected and correct for this dataset.
Fire deficiency file does not carry abuse/neglect categorization — those tags only appear in health deficiency surveys. Always False in this file by design.
Infection control is a health survey category, not fire safety. Always False in fire deficiency file.
Resident rights is a health survey category. Always False in fire deficiency file.
Quality of care is a health survey category. Always False in fire deficiency file.
Date field (YYYY-MM-DD), not numeric. Harness _star heuristic was false-matching _start; heuristic fixed in health_check.py v1.1. No data issue.
Expected 15.1% non-null: only payment denial rows (2,292 of 15,181) have this field. Fines (12,889 rows) have null payment_denial_days. 15.1% = 2292/15181 exactly.
CMS provides a flag indicating whether the MDS measure is a Five-Star input, but has not populated it. 100% null. CMS abandoned field — document in methods note as undocumented behavioral decision.
Claims QM collection period is fixed per vintage: all measures in this pull share 10/01/2024-09/30/2025. Constant by design.
Claims QM collection period is fixed per vintage: all measures in this pull share 10/01/2024-09/30/2025. Constant by design.
CMS provides a flag indicating whether the claims measure is a Five-Star input, but has not populated it. 100% null. Same as MDS QM. CMS abandoned field.
CMS provides collection period dates in QRP dataset but has not populated them. 100% null. The reference_measure_intervals.py crosswalk provides these dates from a separate source.
Same as collection_from — CMS abandoned field in QRP dataset.

datasets.name: pac_puf
datasets.label: Post-Acute Care Public Use File
datasets.note: Pulled with SMRY_CTGRY=PROVIDER filter. Full UUID contains IRF, LTACH, HHA rows â€” re-pull without filter for Phase 3.

known_anomalies.reason: PAC PUF vintage year (2023) — constant within a single-vintage pull.
PAC PUF year type (FY) — constant field indicating fiscal year vintage.
PAC PUF filter used in pull: SMRY_CTGRY=PROVIDER to get facility-level rows only.
PAC PUF filter: SNF-only pull. All rows are SNF. Expand filter for Phase 3 multi-provider analysis.

CMS uses '--' as a suppression sentinel for small cell sizes in PAC PUF. ~54% of cells are '--' (not numeric). Script 06 should coerce '--' -> NaN before writing. Fix before Phase 3 comorbidity/race analysis.
CMS '--' sentinel. See BENE_RRL_PCT note. Same systematic issue.
CMS '--' sentinel. ~68% suppressed. Same systematic issue as BENE_RRL_PCT.
CMS '--' sentinel. Same systematic issue.
CMS '--' sentinel. Same systematic issue.

CMS '--' sentinel. Same systematic issue.
CMS '--' sentinel. 100% suppressed (all values are '--'). This field has no usable data in this pull.
CMS provides this field but has not populated it in recent PAC PUF vintages. 100% null. CMS abandoned field — document in methods note.

datasets.name: snf_cost_report
datasets.label: SNF Cost Report (HCRIS)
datasets.schema_note: Use SNF Days Title XVIII/XIX/Total, NOT Total Days Title X (VA-only days)

known_anomalies.reason: Fire safety subcategory counts in the health survey summary are zero — fire citations are tracked in the separate fire safety dataset, not in health survey summary.
Fire safety subcategory. Zero in health survey summary by design.

CMS batch processing date. Constant within a vintage pull.

datasets.name: snf_vbp_detail
datasets.label: SNF VBP â€
datasets.note: Facility file resolved at runtime via DKAN metastore (284v-j9fz). Contains baseline (FY2022) + performance period (FY2024) columns absent from snf_vbp (05_snf_vbp_national.py) DKAN datastore pull. Aggregate file not in DKAN index; place in input/ for national SNFRM benchmarks.

known_anomalies.reason: Footnote flags are only populated when CMS suppresses a measure; majority of facilities have no suppression so field is mostly empty string (not null). Expected sparse distribution.

datasets.name: nh_state_benchmarks
datasets.label: NH State Benchmarks
datasets.schema_note: National aggregate row uses NATION not US as state identifier

datasets.name: reference_ftag_citations
datasets.label: F-Tag / K-Tag / E-Tag Reference Crosswalk
datasets.note: Built from SOM Appendix PP. Verify against SOM before extending severity stratification.

datasets.name: reference_measure_intervals
datasets.label: QM Measure Code â€” Collection Period Reference
datasets.note: CMS updated 9 measure codes; 2 were mislabeled. Fixed 2026-07-05. Verify any new QM pull.
